title: Profile broadcast sent cleartext to relay before MLS established
description: At connect time, send_profile_broadcast() is called with mls_active=false, so the profile payload is sent as cleartext Broadcast. The relay receives and fans out the raw profile bytes. Trigger: any user with a non-empty profile connecting to the relay. Bad behavior: relay reads user-defined profile fields (can contain arbitrary personal data) in plaintext, violating invariant #3 ('server never sees plaintext message content'). A secondary effect: MLS-active receivers call mls.process_incoming(&payload) on these cleartext bytes, get an error, and silently drop the profile — so peers never see the newcomer's profile until the next UserJoined event re-broadcast (which may also arrive before MLS join, creating the same drop).
